agmarknet.title: District-wise Wholesale Prices (3-month trend) for Rice in Punjab

data.mandi_ai_analysis: The latest monthly modal price for Rice in Ludhiana is ₹2389 per quintal, with a flat 6-month trend. Ludhiana's price is ₹0.34 higher than the state average. There is no change from the previous month, but a 3% increase from the previous year. The field has 97.19% vegetation coverage, with 47.26% healthy and 42.33% stressed; this year's NDVI (0.6233) is lower than the prior years' mean. The stable price trend coincides with a high vegetation coverage and a relatively lower NDVI compared to previous years.

data.news_ai_analysis: Wheat production in India is expected to be between 110 and 120 million tonnes in 2025-26, despite damage from unseasonal rains and hailstorms in key growing states, including Uttar Pradesh. The government has increased its wheat procurement target to 34.5 million tonnes, with procurement already gathering pace in states like Madhya Pradesh. Stubble burning has risen by 23%, with Madhya Pradesh and Uttar Pradesh being the top contributors. New laws for seeds and pesticides are being introduced to curb fake farm inputs and improve agricultural practices.

agmarknet.title: District-wise Wholesale Prices (6-month trend) for Wheat in Uttar Pradesh

data.mandi_ai_analysis: The latest monthly modal price for Wheat in Mathura district is ₹2446.02 per quintal, down 4.12% from October 2025 to March 2026. Mathura's price is ₹18.75 per quintal above the state average. The price dropped 5.2% from February 2026 and 9.1% from March 2025. The field has 89.91% vegetation coverage, with 82.86% healthy and 5.87% stressed; this year's NDVI (0.5838) is above the prior years' mean. The downward price trend coincides with above-average canopy health and vegetation coverage.
